title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Arthur Calgary interviews Mollie Craig, the former wife of Jackie, to discuss the circumstances surrounding Jackie's arrest for the death of his mother. Mollie reveals that her current husband, Joe, works in the power department and had warned her against Jackie, whom she nicknamed "Monkey Face." She describes Jackie as a charming manipulator who once used her money to bribe an auto repair shop owner's wife to drop charges against him.

As Calgary attempts to clear Jackie's name, he finds himself increasingly disgusted by the man's character. Mollie admits that while Jackie claimed he did not intend to kill his mother, he did hit her with a poker during an attempt to get money to avoid prison. She believes Jackie's aggressive nature and need for money led to the incident, though she expresses confusion when Calgary suggests Jackie might be innocent of the crime.

Mollie explains that Jackie kept their marriage secret to hide her from his upper-class family, claiming his mother would try to control her. She recounts a visit to Jackie's family home where a housekeeper refused to believe she was married to him. During this visit, a man named Mr. Aggles offered her a weekly allowance, which she continues to accept despite Joe's disapproval. Mollie notes that Joe provided a sizable check as a wedding gift for her current marriage, hoping it would be more successful than her previous one.
